models.generation_context: G42 generation, introduced 2022; longitudinal-engine, rear-drive CLAR-based platform shared with 3/4 Series. Distinct from the FWD-based F74 2 Series Gran Coupe.
models.model_summary: The BMW 2 Series Coupe is a compact rear-drive luxury coupe on the G42 platform, positioned as BMW's entry-level enthusiast car. The 2026 lineup consists of the 255-hp turbo-four 230i (RWD or xDrive AWD) and the 382-hp turbo inline-six M240i (RWD or xDrive AWD), all paired with an 8-speed ZF automatic. Pricing rose roughly $2,000-$2,400 across the range for 2026, and Comfort Access and wireless device charging are now standard.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (about average, est. 3/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Critics describe the 2026 2 Series Coupe as BMW's most engaging current driver's car at a relatively accessible price, citing the lightweight rear-drive G42 chassis, near-50/50 weight balance, and the well-known ZF 8-speed automatic. The 230i is regarded as a strong, balanced base with adequate performance from its 255-hp B48 turbo four, while the M240i's 382-hp B58 inline-six delivers near-M2 thrust with broader everyday usability. Common complaints include rising prices, a busy iDrive 8.5/Curved Display interface, and a tight rear seat.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner sentiment skews positive: praise focuses on driving dynamics, the inline-six (on M240i), interior quality, and ride. Common complaints concern tight rear-seat space, the complex iDrive 8.5 interface, and the steady price creep.

models.model_summary: The BMW 2 Series Gran Coupe is a compact 4-door sedan built on the FWD-based UKL platform shared with the Mini Countryman and BMW X1. For 2026 the lineup expands with a new front-wheel-drive 228 base trim alongside the existing 228 xDrive and performance-oriented M235 xDrive. All variants use a 2.0L turbocharged inline-four paired to a 7-speed dual-clutch automatic.

Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (about average, est. 3/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the 2 Series Gran Coupe's eager turbo-four powertrain, sharp steering, premium curved-display cabin, and class-leading three years of complimentary maintenance, with the M235 xDrive matching German sport-sedan rivals on real-world acceleration. Common complaints center on a stiff ride over rough pavement, low-speed lurching from the new 7-speed dual-clutch transmission, and limited rear headroom imposed by the coupe-like roofline. The 2026 adds a value-focused FWD 228 base trim that undercuts the Audi A3 on price while delivering more horsepower.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 owner-review sample sizes; Edmunds shows 5/5 stars on 3 reviews. Owners commonly praise responsive handling, fun driving character, and rich feature content while citing fast tire wear and a learning curve with the curved-display infotainment system.

models.notes: The 2 Series Gran Coupe is built on BMW's FWD-based UKL2 platform (shared with the Mini Countryman, BMW X1, and X2) and is distinct from the rear-wheel-drive 2 Series Coupe (CLAR platform). It is BMW's only front-wheel-drive sedan sold in the US. Although BMW markets it as a Gran Coupe, the body is classified as 'sedan' here because it has four conventional doors and a separate trunk. For 2026, BMW added a new FWD-only 228 base trim (replacing the previous AWD-only entry point) to lower the price of entry; the 228 xDrive and M235 xDrive continue largely unchanged from their 2025 redesign. Destination fee of $1,175 confirmed from BMWBlog November 2025 pricing announcement. Images for all trims have needs_scraping:true because BMW USA's consumer site and BMW Group PressClub do not expose direct asset URLs without authentication; follow-up HTML scraping of the product pages will yield CDN asset URLs. Curb weight for the 228 FWD trim is estimated (3,417 lb) based on the typical 100-120 lb savings vs the verified 3,534 lb AWD variant; BMW has not yet published a FWD-specific curb weight.

models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW 3 Series sedan remains the benchmark compact luxury sport sedan, offering a refined balance of precise steering, composed handling, and a premium cabin anchored by BMW's curved 14.9-inch infotainment + 12.3-inch driver display running iDrive 8.5. Two powertrains are offered: the 255-hp turbo-four 330i and the 386-hp turbo-six M340i with 48V mild-hybrid assist. Both come standard rear-wheel-drive or with xDrive all-wheel-drive.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports rates the current-generation 3 Series at roughly average predicted reliability based on 2023-2025 model-year owner data. JD Power has not published a 2026-specific VDS score; the 2025 JD Power Initial Quality Study placed BMW at 196 PP100, slightly worse than the industry average of 192 PP100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 3 Series-specific APEAL score for 2026 MY. Owner sentiment from Edmunds and KBB reviews skews positive on driving dynamics and tech, with some criticism of ride firmness and run-flat tires.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently call the 2026 3 Series the segment benchmark for compact luxury sport sedans, citing precise steering, composed body control, and a polished cabin with a class-leading curved display. The 330i delivers efficient daily-driver performance while the M340i with 48V mild-hybrid I6 offers near-M3 thrust (0-60 in ~4.1 sec) without sacrificing comfort. Critics note the infotainment system can feel menu-heavy and steering feel is somewhat numb compared to older 3 Series generations.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the 3 Series for its sleek styling, fun-to-drive character, refined powertrains, and upscale cabin. Recurring complaints include firm seats on long drives, ride harshness from run-flat tires, and a touchscreen-heavy interface that buries some climate and audio functions.

Pricing reflects standard production trims; BMW also offers a limited 2026 M340i xDrive 50 Jahre Edition at $75,200 + $1,175 destination commemorating 50 years of the 3 Series, not included as a separate trim because it is a single optioned-out package on M340i xDrive. models.model_summary: The BMW 4 Series Coupe is a four-seat, two-door rear-drive (or xDrive AWD) luxury sports coupe built on the G22 platform shared with the 3 Series. For 2026 it returns with four trims spanning the 255 hp turbo 2.0L four-cylinder 430i and the 386 hp turbo 3.0L inline-six M440i with 48V mild-hybrid assist, all paired to a ZF 8-speed automatic. Reviewers consistently praise its balanced chassis, refined powertrains, and BMW's latest Curved Display tech, while the polarizing large vertical-kidney grille and screen-heavy iDrive 8.5 remain common knocks.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (about average, est. 3/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 2026 4 Series Coupe for its agile, well-damped chassis, the smooth and torque-rich B58 inline-six in the M440i, and a comfortable yet sporty character that makes it usable as a daily luxury coupe. Critics flag the divisive oversized vertical kidney grille, a touch-and-menu-heavy iDrive 8.5 interface, and steering that's accurate but light on feedback compared with BMW's storied past. The 430i is rated a sensible base, while the M440i Coupe is repeatedly called the sweet spot of the lineup.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review pages for the 2026 4 Series Coupe specifically had limited sample sizes at time of research; Edmunds aggregates 4 Series owner reviews across body styles, where coupe owners typically praise inline-six performance, steering precision, and refined ride, while citing run-flat tire harshness and complex iDrive menus as the most common complaints.

models.model_summary: The BMW 4 Series Convertible is a four-seat, soft-top luxury convertible based on the G23 platform. It shares its mechanicals with the 4 Series Coupe but adds roughly 450 lb of additional structure for the fabric-top conversion, giving up some acceleration and trunk space in exchange for open-air motoring. The 2026 lineup comprises 430i (turbo 2.0L I4) and M440i (turbo 3.0L I6 with 48V mild hybrid) variants, each available in RWD or xDrive AWD.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the 4 Series Convertible for its refined ride, well-tuned chassis, and the smooth ZF 8-speed automatic. The M440i's B58 turbo inline-six is a highlight for sound and effortless thrust, while the 430i is seen as a sensible base. Common knocks include a heavier feel than the coupe, modest trunk space with the top down, and a busy, screen-heavy iDrive 8.5 interface.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds owner reviews skew strongly positive, with praise for stylish design, comfortable ride, and powerful M440i performance. Common complaints include noisy soft-top operation at speed and complex infotainment.

models.model_summary: The BMW 4 Series Gran Coupe is a four-door fastback derivative of the 4 Series Coupe, sharing its mechanicals with the 3 Series sedan but with a sleeker, lower roofline and a practical liftback hatch. For 2026 it is offered in 430i and M440i forms, each available in rear-wheel-drive or xDrive all-wheel-drive configurations, with all variants using 48V mild-hybrid technology.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (about average, est. 3/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the 4 Series Gran Coupe for its mix of sporty handling, a quiet and well-built cabin, and the practicality of its hatchback trunk versus the more conventional 3 Series sedan. The 430i's mild-hybrid four-cylinder is judged adequate, while the M440i's inline-six is highlighted as a smooth and rapid powertrain. Polarizing front-grille styling and an option-heavy pricing structure (notably adaptive cruise as part of an extra-cost Driving Assistance Professional package) are the most common complaints.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review aggregate ratings and sample sizes from Edmunds and KBB could not be reliably extracted (both sites returned 403 to our requests). Edmunds' editorial summary reports that owner sentiment is positive, with common praise focused on the car's sportiness, premium feel, and hatchback practicality, and common criticism centered on the polarizing front-end styling and the cost of optional driver-assistance packages.

models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW 5 Series is the 8th-generation (G60) midsize luxury sedan, offered in the US with three powertrain choices: a 2.0L turbo four-cylinder (530i/530i xDrive), a 3.0L turbo inline-six (540i xDrive), and a plug-in hybrid (550e xDrive) combining the inline-six with an electric motor. All ICE trims use a 48V mild-hybrid system. The 2026 update is minor, adding new interior color options, an exterior color (Frozen Portimao Blue), and small package price adjustments.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports describes the 2026 5 Series as expected to have about-average reliability based on the 2024-2025 model data and the BMW brand history. No segment-level JD Power VDS PP100 has been published for the 5 Series specifically for this MY; BMW as a brand ranked 9th overall and 3rd among luxury automakers in the 2024 JD Power VDS (190 PP100).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the G60 5 Series for refined turbocharged powertrains, a quiet and composed ride, a high-quality cabin, and BMW's vast curved display with iDrive 8.5. Criticism centers on the heavy reliance on touchscreen controls for HVAC and basic functions and the loss of some traditional tactile buttons. The 540i xDrive is widely cited as the value sweet spot, while the 550e PHEV draws praise for near-M-level acceleration combined with 33 miles of EPA electric range.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds owner reviews of the current-generation 5 Series are generally positive, praising strong performance, comfortable ride, styling, and tech. Common complaints focus on touch-only controls and limited physical buttons, plus the steep learning curve of iDrive 8.5. Edmunds notes the 2026 model year has a small sample size and pools recent G60 reviews.

models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW 7 Series is BMW's full-size flagship luxury sedan, a carry-over year ahead of a 2027 mid-cycle refresh. The lineup spans a 375-hp turbo inline-six (740i / 740i xDrive), a 483-hp plug-in hybrid (750e xDrive) with roughly 34 miles of EPA electric range, and a 536-hp twin-turbo V8 (760i xDrive). All trims share the polarizing split-headlight G70 face, BMW Curved Display, and access to the optional 31.3-inch 8K rear Theatre Screen.

reliability.summary: JD Power's 2026 VDS does not separately profile the current-generation 7 Series, and Consumer Reports does not publish a predicted-reliability score for the 2026 model. Anecdotal owner data on Edmunds and KBB skews positive on drivetrain refinement but flags repeated complaints about the iDrive 8.5 software and electronics quality. Industry-wide, BMW ranked 9th in the 2025 VDS with 189 PP100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the 2026 7 Series as a carry-over model that still defines BMW's high-tech, performance-leaning take on the flagship sedan, with smooth mild-hybrid and PHEV powertrains and a thunderous V8 in the 760i. The split-headlight styling and dense touch-based interior controls remain the most frequent complaints, and the 7 Series often loses comfort comparisons to the Mercedes-Benz S-Class. Edmunds rates it 7.4/10, citing strong acceleration and tech against polarizing design and complex UX.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the smooth, comfortable ride, opulent materials and rear-seat experience, but consistently complain that the iDrive 8.5 system and touch-based controls are confusing, and that the car depreciates quickly. Several note electronics and assembly quality issues.

Many spec blocks (safety crash ratings, JD Power VDS, NHTSA overall) are null because the 2026 7 Series has not yet been crash-tested or profiled by these agencies; the segment is low-volume and rarely tested. models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW X1 is a compact luxury SUV offered in the US in two ICE trims: the xDrive28i and the higher-output M35i. Both use a turbocharged 2.0-liter four-cylinder paired with a 7-speed dual-clutch transmission and standard xDrive all-wheel drive. For 2026 the lineup gains wireless phone charging, auto-dimming mirrors, and Comfort Access as standard, along with new exterior colors.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports 2026 predicts about average reliability for this model (3/5 on CR's scale). Cross-checked against published CR 2026 reliability page.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rate the 2026 X1 as one of the better entry-level luxury compact SUVs, citing its premium-feeling cabin, BMW Curved Display tech, strong turbocharged engine, and composed handling. Common gripes are a sometimes harsh stop-start system, occasionally hesitant dual-clutch tuning, and a firm ride on the optional adaptive M Sport suspension. The M35i adds meaningful straight-line speed but most buyers will be satisfied with the xDrive28i.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds shows roughly 43 consumer reviews for the 2026 BMW X1 (rating value not extracted directly due to site access restrictions). Common owner praise centers on interior quality, technology, and the engine; complaints focus on transmission tuning, ride firmness with sport wheels, and stop-start behavior.

models.model_summary: The BMW X2 is a coupe-styled compact luxury SUV (BMW markets it as a Sports Activity Coupe). All US trims pair a turbocharged 2.0-liter inline-four with a 7-speed dual-clutch automatic and standard xDrive all-wheel drive. The 2026 update adds wireless phone charging, power-folding heated mirrors, and Comfort Access keyless entry as standard while reducing prices on the Convenience and Technology option packages.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (about average, est. 3/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the X2's sharp styling, refined turbo-four powertrains, and updated BMW Curved Display with iDrive 9, but knock its rear-seat headroom, choppy ride on the M35i, and the menu-deep OS 9 interface. Most see the xDrive28i as the value pick since the M35i adds roughly $9,000 for performance many buyers won't fully use.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise exterior styling, turbo powertrain pull, and AWD confidence; common complaints center on a glitchy OS 9 touchscreen, tight rear headroom from the sloping roof, and stiff ride on the M35i with 20-inch wheels.

models.generation_context: 4th gen (G45), introduced for 2025 MY
models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW X3 is the second model year of the all-new fourth-generation (G45) midsize luxury SUV. The US lineup consists of two trims, both all-wheel drive with a 48V mild-hybrid system: the X3 30 xDrive with a 2.0L turbo four-cylinder making 255 hp, and the X3 M50 xDrive with a 3.0L turbo inline-six making 393 hp. The new generation introduces a sleeker exterior, a 14.9-inch curved infotainment display with iDrive 9, and adopts BMW's new simplified naming convention (X3 30 xDrive in place of the previous xDrive30i).

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports projects the 2026 X3 to have about-average reliability based on the 2025 model year, the first year of the G45 generation. No model-specific JD Power VDS PP100 has been published for the G45 X3 at time of research. BMW has issued one NHTSA recall on the 2026 X3 for a steering-system software update (free, OTA or dealer).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the G45 X3 as the smartest pick in the compact luxury SUV class, citing its composed ride, strong powertrains, expansive 14.9-inch curved display with iDrive 9, and impressive build quality for the price. Edmunds and Car and Driver both recommend the 30 xDrive as the value sweet spot, noting that the M50's ~$15,000 premium is hard to justify unless performance is a priority. The most common critique is the touchscreen-only HVAC controls and a base interior with some hard plastics that feel below the price point.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds owner sentiment on the 2026 X3 is mixed but trends positive: praise centers on responsive handling, strong powertrains, and the modern cabin tech, while common complaints focus on the touchscreen-driven climate controls requiring multiple steps and some less-premium plastics for the price. Sample size is small as the model year is still ramping.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen G05 LCI (introduced as MY2024 LCI refresh, current as of MY2026)
models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW X5 is a midsize luxury SUV with rear- or all-wheel drive, offering inline-six MHEV, PHEV, and V8 powertrains. It is the LCI (mid-cycle) refresh of the 4th-generation G05, carrying over for 2026 with no major changes ahead of an all-new G65 successor. The lineup spans the sDrive40i and xDrive40i with a 375-hp turbo I6, the xDrive50e PHEV with ~40 mi of electric range, and the M60i with a 523-hp twin-turbo V8.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports 2026 predicts above average reliability for this model (4/5 on CR's scale). Cross-checked against published CR 2026 reliability page.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rank the 2026 X5 among the strongest midsize luxury SUVs, citing a composed and quiet ride, strong six-cylinder and V8 powertrains, and refined interior materials. The xDrive50e is singled out for its 483 hp combined output and an expanded ~40-mile electric range, while criticisms focus on the complexity of iDrive 8.5 and a firm ride on the largest optional wheels. Most outlets note that 2026 carries over unchanged ahead of the upcoming next-gen G65.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds lists 35 consumer reviews for the 2026 X5 but did not surface an overall star average in the reachable HTML. Common praise across recent X5 owner commentary focuses on power, ride comfort, and interior luxury; recurring complaints involve iDrive complexity and run-flat tire ride harshness on larger wheels.

models.model_summary: The BMW X6 is a midsize luxury coupe-style SUV that shares its platform and powertrains with the X5 but trades upright SUV practicality for a sharply sloped fastback roofline. For 2026 it is offered in two ICE trims with 48V mild-hybrid assist: the inline-six xDrive40i and the V8-powered M60i. All X6s come standard with xDrive AWD, an 8-speed automatic, the BMW Curved Display (12.3-inch driver + 14.9-inch infotainment) running iDrive 8.5, and the M Sport package.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (about average, est. 3/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the X6 for its strong powertrains, polished interior, and twin-screen Curved Display, with the M60i V8 singled out as the sweet spot for performance. Critics knock the X6 for sacrificing X5 cargo and rear headroom for the coupe roofline with little dynamic benefit — MotorTrend characterizes it as a vehicle with a 'split personality.' The 2026 model is a carryover with only minor color/feature updates.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds owners praise the driving experience, design, luxury feel, and standout twin-screen tech; common gripes elsewhere center on rear visibility and reduced cargo/headroom compared to the X5. KBB rating not accessible during research.

Seven seats are standard; six-seat captain's chairs are optional. It pairs a refined ride with a luxurious cabin and the BMW Curved Display running iDrive 8.5.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the X7 for handling that belies its size, a plush cabin with premium materials, and strong powertrains, while noting that the inline-six is more than sufficient for most buyers and the V8 is buttery smooth but thirsty. owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the X7's smooth ride, spacious interior, and powerful engines, often describing it as luxurious and fun to drive. Common complaints involve infotainment/technology glitches, brake noise, and inconsistent dealer service experiences.

models.model_summary: The BMW ALPINA XB7 is a three-row full-size luxury SUV based on the BMW X7 (G07) platform, hand-finished by ALPINA with a unique 631-hp twin-turbocharged 4.4L V8, 8-speed Sport Automatic, and xDrive AWD. Marketed by BMW USA as a distinct model with a single trim, the XB7 emphasizes high-speed grand-touring capability and bespoke ALPINA interior trim over the more performance-aggressive M-branded BMW SUVs. 2026 is the final year of XB7 production; BMW (which now owns ALPINA) is closing the chapter with a 120-unit MANUFAKTUR special edition.

professional_reviews.summary: Professional reviewers consistently praise the XB7 as one of the most refined and capable full-size luxury SUVs available, with effortless 631-hp acceleration, autobahn-stable composure, and a more understated luxury character than the BMW XM. Common criticisms note that traction control intervenes aggressively when pushed in corners, fuel economy is modest at 17 mpg combined, and the third row falls short of dedicated executive-class rivals like the Bentayga EWB.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds lists a small number of owner reviews (4 for 2026, 3 for 2024) but the displayed aggregate numeric star rating could not be confirmed. Common owner praise centers on the V8 powertrain, Bowers & Wilkins audio system, and ride quality; complaints are sparse given the low volume but include the price premium over a regular X7 M60i.

models.model_summary: The BMW XM is the M division's halo PHEV SAV and the first standalone M-branded model since the M1. For 2026, BMW consolidated the lineup to a single XM Label trim producing 738 hp from a twin-turbo 4.4L V8 paired with an electric motor and 19.2 kWh battery, offering ~30 miles of EPA electric range and 0-60 mph in 3.6 seconds. It is a low-volume, ultra-premium offering competing with Mercedes-AMG GLE 63 S and Lamborghini Urus.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the XM Label's prodigious 738 hp output, M-tuned chassis with active anti-roll, and bespoke interior with twin-screen curved display and B&W Diamond audio. Common criticism centers on high road and tire noise that compromises the luxury experience, polarizing exterior styling, and a >6,000 lb curb weight that blunts the agility expected of an M-branded vehicle. Edmunds scored the 2026 XM Label 5.7/10 in expert testing.

models.model_summary: The BMW Z4 is a 2-seat rear-wheel-drive soft-top roadster offered in two trims: the turbo-four sDrive30i and the turbo-six M40i. It shares its platform with the Toyota GR Supra and is the only roadster — and the only car offering a manual transmission — in BMW's current US lineup. 2026 is the final model year; production at Magna Steyr in Graz, Austria ends in March 2026.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a Vehicle Dependability Study score specifically for the 2026 Z4 as of May 2026; the 2025 Z4 received a JD Power Quality & Reliability rating in the 'Great' band (around 85/100). Consumer Reports predicts BMW brand reliability around average. Low-volume specialty roadster — limited longitudinal data.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the 2026 Z4 as a carryover model whose chassis and powertrains remain genuinely engaging: the M40i with the Handschalter 6-speed manual draws particular praise for restoring sports-car feel after a six-year automatic-only run. The four-cylinder sDrive30i is rated solid but less special. Common gripes are tight ingress/egress, dated iDrive 7 software, and a small trunk; reviewers note this is the last opportunity to buy a new BMW roadster.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the Z4's styling, fun-to-drive character, refined ride, comfortable seats, high-quality interior, and effective 8-speed automatic with strong fuel economy for the segment. Common complaints are difficult ingress/egress due to low roofline and significant brake dust.

A new M2 xDrive variant has been announced for the 2027 model year.

models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW M3 is the high-performance variant of the 3 Series sedan, powered by BMW M's twin-turbocharged 3.0L S58 inline-six. Three configurations are offered in the US: a 473-hp manual-only rear-drive base model, a 503-hp Competition with 8-speed automatic and rear drive, and a 523-hp Competition xDrive with all-wheel drive. The G80 generation is in its mid-cycle refresh phase, and 2026 brings a heated steering wheel as standard plus standard remote start on Competition trims; the M3 Touring wagon remains Europe-only.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the M3's S58 inline-six for ferocious thrust and a broad torque band, with the Competition xDrive delivering 0-60 mph in roughly 3.0-3.2 seconds in independent testing. The G80 platform earns high marks for chassis composure, all-day grip, and a genuinely usable daily-driver trunk and rear seat. Common criticisms are polarizing front-end styling, dense iDrive 8.5 menus on the curved touchscreen, and steering feel that some testers find numb compared with prior M3 generations.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the M3's powerful engine, accessible daily-driver usability, and luxury-grade interior, with several specifically calling out the six-speed manual on the base trim as a highlight. Common complaints center on the size and weight of the current G80 generation versus earlier M3s, polarizing front styling, and an infotainment system that some find overly menu-driven.

models.model_summary: The BMW M4 Convertible is the soft-top, four-seat dedicated M-division version of the 4 Series Convertible. For 2026 it is offered in the US in a single configuration — Competition with M xDrive — pairing a 523-hp twin-turbo S58 inline-six with an eight-speed automatic, AWD with a rear-biased character, and an electrically operated fabric roof. With the 8 Series Convertible discontinued, it is BMW's flagship M convertible.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (about average, est. 3/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 523-hp Competition xDrive Convertible for its blistering 3.6-second 0-60 mph time, refined drop-top experience with the lighter fabric roof, and full M-spec chassis hardware including adaptive M dampers and Active M differential. Common criticisms include the polarizing styling, the lack of a manual transmission option (Convertible is auto-only), and steering that's deliberate and quick but somewhat synthetic in feel. Edmunds gave the broader 2026 M4 lineup a 7.6/10 expert score.

owner_reviews.summary: 2026-MY owner reviews are sparse this early in the model year. Sentiment carried over from 2024-2025 M4 owners is generally positive on performance and exclusivity, with frequent complaints about iDrive complexity, ride harshness in firmer drive modes, and aggressive seat bolsters on optional M Carbon buckets.

models.generation_context: G90, all-new for 2025 MY; first plug-in hybrid M5
models.model_summary: The G90 BMW M5 Sedan is the seventh-generation M5 and the first ever with a plug-in hybrid powertrain. A 4.4L twin-turbo V8 paired with an electric motor between the engine and 8-speed automatic produces 717 hp combined and 738 lb-ft of torque, driven through rear-biased M xDrive AWD. EPA-rated for ~25 miles of all-electric driving from a 14.8 kWh (usable) battery, the M5 is also the heaviest M5 ever at roughly 5,390 lb.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (about average, est. 3/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the G90 M5's straight-line ferocity, the seamless hybrid integration, and the lavish interior with BMW's curved display, while consistently noting that the ~5,400 lb curb weight blunts the agility and feel that defined earlier M5 generations. The car is widely viewed as a deeply capable daily-driver supersedan that prioritizes luxury and tech over the visceral edge of the F90 it replaces.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner sample sizes on Edmunds and KBB for the 2026 M5 Sedan are too small to report reliably; early owner sentiment emphasizes blistering acceleration and interior tech while complaining about weight, soft on-limit feedback, and the M5's departure from a traditional driver-focused character.

models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW M5 Touring is the first M5 wagon ever sold in the US, pairing the G90 M5 Sedan's 717-hp twin-turbo 4.4L V8 PHEV powertrain with a five-door wagon body. It targets enthusiast buyers who want extreme M-division performance combined with the cargo practicality and daily-driver versatility of a long-roof estate. With a ~5,400 lb curb weight it is heavier than the sedan, but offers the same drivetrain, AWD, and 8-speed automatic.

professional_reviews.summary: Professional reviewers celebrate the M5 Touring's historic US debut and its combination of 717-hp PHEV performance with wagon practicality (27.2 cu-ft behind the rear seats), while criticizing the ~5,400 lb curb weight that blunts the chassis feel of prior M5s. Car and Driver recorded a 3.1-second 0-60 mph time in instrumented testing despite EPA-rated 25-mile electric range, and reviewers consistently note that the Touring is the more usable but less focused interpretation of the M5 formula.

owner_reviews.summary: No aggregated owner-review data is yet available for the 2026 M5 Touring on Edmunds or KBB; the vehicle is too new and the sample size is insufficient.

Power goes through an 8-speed M Steptronic with integrated electric motor to a rear-biased M xDrive AWD system with Active M Differential, delivering 0-60 mph in a manufacturer-claimed 3.7 seconds. It is distinct from the X5 M60i (an M Performance trim within the X5 model line) and shares its V8 platform with the X6 M Competition and (re-tuned) the M5 G90.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently describe the LCI X5 M Competition as one of the quickest and most capable performance SUVs available, with the new 48V mild-hybrid S68 V8 delivering even more immediate response than the outgoing S63. Most praise the straight-line ferocity, all-weather usability of M xDrive, and the upgraded Curved Display interior, while noting that the firm ride, heft (~5,500 lb), and tire wear remain compromises inherent to the format. Edmunds rates the 2026 X5 M 6.8 out of 10 in expert testing.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds owner reviews average roughly 4 out of 5 stars across a small sample of about 11 reviews of recent X5 M models. Owners commonly praise the explosive acceleration, all-weather traction, and luxury interior, while complaints focus on aggressive seat bolstering, complex iDrive controls, and rapid tire wear with expensive replacement costs.

models.model_summary: The X6 M Competition is the full-fat M version of BMW's coupe-styled mid-size SUV, sharing its 617-hp twin-turbo 4.4L V8 (S68) with 48V mild-hybrid assistance and M xDrive AWD with the X5 M Competition. It is sold in a single trim only and trades some of the X5 M's cargo space and headroom for a sloped coupe-SUV roofline.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently describe the X6 M Competition as one of the quickest gas-powered SUVs on sale, with brutally fast acceleration, sharp steering and prodigious grip from its M xDrive system, but a stiff ride and a smaller, lower-roof cargo area than the mechanically identical X5 M Competition. The polarizing coupe-SUV styling and six-figure price are seen as the main reasons to choose it over the X5 M; otherwise the cars drive nearly identically.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners on Edmunds rate the 2026 X6 M five stars on a small sample (7 reviews, which Edmunds notes is supplemented with reviews of recent prior MYs). Common praise focuses on outright pace, V8 sound and luxury interior; some shorter drivers find ingress/egress awkward and ride comfort is the most common complaint.

models.model_summary: The BMW i4 is an all-electric Gran Coupe (4-door fastback liftback) built on the same CLAR platform as the ICE 4 Series Gran Coupe. For 2026 it carries over from 2025 with no price changes, gains modest range improvements from new silicon-carbide inverters, and replaces the prior M50 with a more powerful M60 performance variant. Three trims are offered in the US: eDrive40 (RWD), xDrive40 (AWD), and M60 (M-tuned AWD).

No major recurring defects reported for current MY.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the i4 for retaining traditional BMW driving dynamics in an EV package, with a high-quality interior, strong range (eDrive40 up to 333 mi), and quick acceleration across the lineup. Common criticisms target the touchscreen-heavy iDrive 8.5 interface, a tight rear seat, and a price that runs well above the Tesla Model 3 and Hyundai Ioniq 6. The 2026 M60 (replacing M50) cuts the 0-60 time to 3.6 sec.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds shows 44 consumer reviews for the 2025 i4 (most recent year with substantial owner sample); current-MY-specific star average not extracted due to Edmunds 403 blocking. Owners praise instant acceleration, smooth ride, and luxurious interior; complaints center on confusing infotainment, cold M steering wheel in winter, small blind-spot warning lights, and a rear-camera lens that gets dirty quickly.

Built in Munich, Germany. 2026 MY is a carryover with three key changes: (1) silicon-carbide inverters give modest range bumps (~5-15 mi depending on trim/wheel), (2) M50 renamed to M60 with ~57 hp increase to 593 hp combined Sport mode, (3) BMW explicitly excluded i4 (and other EVs) from the 2026 brand-wide price increase, so MSRP unchanged from 2025. 2026 i4 retains CCS1 port; BMW transitioning to native NACS starting with i5 M60 and continuing with iX3, so i4's NACS native integration is later. models.generation_context: G60-platform i5 (1st-gen all-electric 5 Series), introduced for 2024 MY; 2026 brings efficiency-driven range gains and standard Harman Kardon audio
models.model_summary: The BMW i5 is the all-electric variant of the G60 5 Series sedan, sharing its body and chassis with the gas/PHEV 5 Series. For 2026 it adds silicon-carbide inverter components and lower-friction wheel bearings that lift EPA range by up to 15 miles on the base eDrive40, and Harman Kardon audio is now standard across the lineup. Three trims are sold in the US: a single-motor RWD eDrive40, a dual-motor AWD xDrive40, and a 593-hp dual-motor M60 xDrive performance variant from BMW M.
models.notes: All-electric 5 Series sibling, G60 platform. i5 Touring (wagon) not offered in US. M60 xDrive is the performance variant (~593 hp). Built in Dingolfing, Germany. Highway Assistant offers hands-free driving up to 85 mph on mapped highways. 2026 i5 M60 ships with native NACS charge port from the factory; eDrive40 and xDrive40 transitioned to native NACS during the 2026 model year (cars built from March 2026 onward have NACS). For 2026, all three trims have a usable battery capacity of approximately 81.2 kWh (total ~84.3 kWh). Sources: bmwusa.com, fueleconomy.gov, press.bmwgroup.com, IIHS, bmwblog.com.

reliability.summary: JD Power VDS does not yet score the i5 specifically. Consumer Reports gives the i5 about-average expected reliability for its model year; early data noted multiple recalls on the 2024 launch model (mostly software-addressable) but otherwise solid powertrain performance. Not enough field data exists for 2026 to confirm a confident long-term score.

No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rank the i5 as the most engaging EV sedan in its class, citing precise steering, composed body control and a refined cabin. MotorTrend recorded the eDrive40 at 5.1 seconds to 60 mph and recommended the 19-inch-wheel eDrive40 for maximum range. Consumer Reports called the M60 one of the best cars they have tested, electric or otherwise, praising its quickness, agility and serene ride.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner sentiment on Edmunds and KBB is overwhelmingly positive: praise focuses on ride/handling balance, tech, comfort, and overall premium feel; a recurring complaint is the relatively shallow trunk and rear footwell space (a compromise of the shared ICE/EV platform). Aggregate star ratings are not consistently exposed in public summaries.

models.generation_context: G70-based all-electric variant, introduced 2023; eDrive50 RWD added for 2025 MY
models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW i7 is the all-electric flagship sedan sibling to the ICE/PHEV 7 Series, sharing the G70 platform and luxury cabin. Three trims are offered: the single-motor RWD eDrive50 (449 hp), dual-motor AWD xDrive60 (536 hp), and the M-developed M70 xDrive (650 hp), which BMW positions as its most powerful production EV. All trims share a ~105.7 kWh (101.7 kWh usable) battery, EPA ranges of 268-321 miles depending on trim and wheel choice, and the BMW Curved Display, with the 31.3-inch 8K Theatre Screen available as an option for rear passengers.

reliability.summary: Neither JD Power's 2026 VDS nor Consumer Reports publishes a model-specific predicted-reliability score for the 2026 BMW i7. EV-specific reliability concerns reported by owners on Edmunds and BMW forums cluster around iDrive 8.5 software glitches, occasional 12V battery drain on parked cars, and 2023-MY production door-actuator recalls; the powertrain itself has been comparatively trouble-free. BMW overall ranked 9th in the 2025 JD Power VDS at 189 PP100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the 2026 i7 as a quiet, plush, technology-forward luxury sedan that wins on rear-seat experience, refinement and straight-line acceleration but loses ground to rivals on driving engagement and outright range. The polarizing split-headlight G70 styling and dense touch-heavy iDrive 8.5 interface remain the most frequent complaints. The M70 xDrive is praised for stunning straight-line performance (3.5 sec 0-60) but criticized for trading range for power versus a Lucid Air Sapphire or Porsche Taycan Turbo S.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review samples for the 2026 i7 specifically are too small to score reliably; aggregate reviews of the 2023-2025 i7 lineup on Edmunds and KBB praise the silent, smooth ride, opulent interior and Theatre Screen, while complaining about iDrive complexity, occasional software glitches, range below class leaders, and high depreciation.

models.generation_context: 1st gen (I20), introduced for 2022 MY; received significant LCI mid-cycle refresh for 2026 MY (debuted January 2025, on sale Q2 2025) with new naming (xDrive45/60/M70), bigger batteries, revised exterior, silicon-carbide inverters, and updated interior.
models.model_summary: The 2026 BMW iX is a midsize all-electric Sports Activity Vehicle and BMW's flagship EV until the Neue Klasse iX3 arrives. The 2026 model year is a major mid-cycle refresh: the lineup is renamed (xDrive45 replaces the previous xDrive50 as a new lower-priced base, xDrive60 replaces xDrive50 at the next tier, and M70 xDrive replaces the M60), all trims get larger batteries with silicon-carbide inverters for improved range and efficiency, exterior styling is updated (new grille frame, redesigned LED headlights), and the cabin gets refreshed materials and BMW Operating System 8.5. All trims are dual-motor AWD-electric using BMW's 5th-generation eDrive; range tops out at 364 EPA miles on the xDrive60 with 20-inch wheels.

reliability.summary: No 2026 model-specific reliability data is published yet. The iX has been on sale in the US since the 2022 MY, and the prior generation has shown an average to slightly below-average reliability picture in Consumer Reports tracking, with the heaviest complaints centered on infotainment glitches and 12V battery issues. The 2026 refresh introduces new silicon-carbide inverters, revised thermal management, and software updates whose reliability impact is unknown. BMW as a brand ranked 9th overall and 3rd among luxury automakers in the 2024 JD Power VDS (190 PP100).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Critics broadly agree that the 2026 iX is one of the most well-rounded luxury electric SUVs, praising its remarkably quiet and composed ride, supple air suspension, strong real-world range (often exceeding EPA estimates), and the addition of a newly affordable xDrive45 base trim. The 2026 refresh also draws praise for incrementally better efficiency thanks to silicon-carbide inverters, and most reviewers consider the xDrive60 the value sweet spot of the lineup. Common criticism centers on the polarizing front-end styling (the large kidney grille on an EV without an engine), 400V architecture that lags newer competitors in DC fast charging speed despite peaks of 175-195 kW, and a touch-heavy iDrive 8.5 interface with relatively few physical controls.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds aggregates roughly 30 owner reviews for the 2026 iX with overall positive sentiment; owners praise strong power, smooth driving, comfortable interior, and abundant cargo space. Common complaints focus on touch-heavy controls, the polarizing exterior styling, and DC fast charging speeds that are slower than newer EV competitors above 80% state of charge. Exact star ratings were not retrievable from a 403-blocked Edmunds page at time of research.

Image asset URLs are from BMW's scene7 CDN (bmw.scene7.com) and were extracted from bmwusa.com/vehicles/bmw-i-series/ix/bmw-ix.html as direct image asset paths. models.generation_context: All-new 2nd-gen iX3 (internal code NA5), 1st BMW Neue Klasse production model; sized similarly to the X3 and built on the new 6th-gen eDrive 800V platform.
models.model_summary: The 2027 BMW iX3 50 xDrive is BMW's first Neue Klasse production vehicle, an all-electric midsize SAV launching in the US in September 2026 as a single-trim model. It uses BMW's 6th-generation eDrive technology with 800V architecture, cylindrical 4695-format NMC cells, a 108.7 kWh battery (~108 kWh usable), and a dual-motor AWD layout producing 463 hp and 476 lb-ft. EPA-rated range is 383-434 miles depending on wheel/tire configuration; DC fast charging peaks at 400 kW (10-80% in approximately 21 minutes) through a standard NACS port.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the bmw brand at 198 PP100 (lower is better) — better than the 204 industry average and better than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (about average (new model, brand fallback), est. 3/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Early professional reviews of the 2027 iX3 50 xDrive (largely first-drive impressions from European-market prototypes and US-spec preview drives) are highly positive. Reviewers consistently praise the Panoramic iDrive heads-up display spanning the base of the windshield, the 800V architecture enabling rapid charging, strong straight-line performance from the dual-motor setup, and a meaningful range advantage over BMW's previous EVs. Common criticisms cite the heavy reliance on touch and voice controls and a steep learning curve for the new BMW Operating System X interface.

owner_reviews.summary: No owner reviews exist for the 2027 BMW iX3. US deliveries are scheduled to begin in late September 2026; the configurator and reservation system opened in May 2026 with a $1,000 refundable deposit.

trims.notes: Single launch trim of the all-new Neue Klasse iX3. BMW USA designates as 2027 MY despite launching in mid-2026; US configurator and $1,000 refundable reservations went live in May 2026 with deliveries beginning late September 2026. Powertrain output of 463 hp/476 lb-ft is the manufacturer-stated combined dual-motor figure available in Sport mode; some non-US references quote 469 hp or 345 kW. Battery is 108.7 kWh gross / approximately 108 kWh usable using new 4695-format cylindrical NMC cells. EPA range varies by configuration: 383 mi on standard 20-in all-season tires (102 MPGe) up to 434 mi on 20-in summer tires (118 MPGe); conservative 383 mi used here for electric_range_mi and 434 mi used for total_range_mi to capture the full EPA range. Charging is via standard NACS port with included CCS adapter; this is BMW's first US-market vehicle with NACS native. AC charging supports 11 kW Level 2 (15.4 kW available via BMW Wallbox Professional with 3-phase service rarely used in US; 11 kW is the standard US value). 0-60 is 4.7 sec per BMW; some independent testers report sub-4.7. Top speed 130 mph is electronically limited and unverified for US trim. NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested the 2027 iX3 as of research date. EPA fueleconomy.gov listing not yet live for 2027 iX3 at research date; URL in sources is forward-looking. Cargo: 19.1 cu ft behind 2nd row (540L), 56.5 cu ft max with seats folded (1,600L) per converted European figures; US-specific figures not yet published. Includes 58L (~2 cu ft) front trunk not separately captured in schema. Standard ADAS booleans reflect Driving Assistant Plus contents per BMW; adaptive cruise control is included as standard on this trim (unlike some other BMW models where it's optional). Built at BMW Plant Debrecen, Hungary.

models.notes: First BMW Neue Klasse production model. 6th-gen eDrive with 800V architecture, NACS port at launch with included CCS adapter. Currently reservation-only with $1,000 refundable deposit; US deliveries begin fall 2026 (September 25, 2026 launch date). BMW USA designates as 2027 MY despite mid-2026 launch. Built at BMW Plant Debrecen, Hungary (first BMW plant to run on 100% renewable energy in normal operation, also the site of BMW's first Gen6 high-voltage battery facility); North American production may eventually shift to BMW's San Luis Potosi (Mexico) plant when it becomes operational mid-2027. Production X3-sized SUV with up to ~434 mi EPA range, replacing the previous G08 iX3 not previously sold in US (this is the first US iX3). The 2026 launch is single-trim (iX3 50 xDrive); additional trims (iX3 40, iX3 M70) are expected later in the lifecycle but are not on sale in the US as of May 2026. No professional review-derived reliability or owner data exists yet. Images are from the BMW USA consumer site scene7 CDN; the page uses 'MY27' and 'SOC26' (start-of-cycle 2026) asset name patterns consistent with the 2027-MY designation.
